text.title: Methodology Corpus Licence Bundle — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `methodology-corpus-licence-bundle` MUST enforce. Each rule carries an empirical anchor under `source`.

rule.statement: Licence bundle MUST enumerate (tiers, domains, slug count) in scope. «All content» is rejected.
rule.rationale: Vague scope creates downstream disputes about what was paid for; explicit scope is the only auditable artefact.

rule.statement: Bundle MUST state redistribution stance — internal use only, may be redistributed within named subsidiary, may be re-licensed. Pick one.
rule.rationale: Without explicit redistribution clause, customers default to whatever interpretation suits them; disputes become legal cases.

rule.statement: Bundle MUST state attribution requirement (e.g., «Powered by Faion methodology corpus v1.x») and where it appears.
rule.rationale: Without attribution, downstream users have no path back to Faion for upgrades / corrections.

rule.statement: Bundle MUST name the audit hook — log-line, telemetry endpoint, or quarterly self-attestation — that proves usage stayed in scope.
rule.rationale: Without an audit hook, scope compliance is voluntary; auditors will reject the bundle as non-binding.

rule.statement: Bundle MUST set renewal cadence + named approver. Open-ended licences are rejected.
rule.rationale: Open-ended licences accumulate forever; named renewal forces a periodic deal-level review.
